---
title: The Role of Stratospheric Polar Vortex Breakdown in Southern Hemisphere Climate
  Trends
pagetitle: 2014-7-1-Sun2014 - Gang Chen
body-classes: publication-page
format:
  html:
    title-block-style: none
year: 2014
author: Lantao Sun, Gang Chen and Walter A. Robinson
venue: Journal of the Atmospheric Sciences
venue_cit: <i>Journal of the Atmospheric Sciences</i>, 71, 2335--2353, doi:10.1175/JAS-D-13-0290.1.
recommended_citation: 'Lantao Sun, Gang Chen and Walter A. Robinson, 2014: The Role
  of Stratospheric Polar Vortex Breakdown in Southern Hemisphere Climate Trends, <i>Journal
  of the Atmospheric Sciences</i>, 71, 2335--2353, doi:10.1175/JAS-D-13-0290.1.'
paper_url: http://journals.ametsoc.org/doi/abs/10.1175/JAS-D-13-0290.1
abstract: This paper investigates the connection between the delay in the final breakdown
  of the stratospheric polar vortex, the stratospheric final warming (SFW), and Southern
  Hemisphere climate trends. We first analyze ERA-Interim reanalysis and three climate
  model outputs with different climate forcings. Climate trends appear when there
  is a delay in the timing of SFWs. When regressed onto the SFW dates (which reflects
  the anomaly when the SFW is delayed for one standard deviation of its onset dates),
  the anomaly pattern bears a resemblance to the observed climate trends, for all
  the model outputs, even without any trends. This suggests that the stratospheric
  and tropospheric circulations are organized by the timing of SFWs, in both the interannual
  time scale and climate trends due to external forcings.We further explore the role
  of the SFW using a simplified dynamical model, in which the ozone depletion is mimicked
  by a springtime polar stratospheric cooling. The responses of zonal-mean atmospheric
  circ...
---

::: {.page-with-sidebar}

::: {.sidebar-col}
{{< include /_author-profile.qmd >}}
:::

::: {.content-col}

# The Role of Stratospheric Polar Vortex Breakdown in Southern Hemisphere Climate Trends

*Published in Journal of the Atmospheric Sciences, 2014*

**Recommended citation:** Lantao Sun, Gang Chen and Walter A. Robinson, 2014: The Role of Stratospheric Polar Vortex Breakdown in Southern Hemisphere Climate Trends, <i>Journal of the Atmospheric Sciences</i>, 71, 2335--2353, doi:10.1175/JAS-D-13-0290.1.

## Abstract

This paper investigates the connection between the delay in the final breakdown of the stratospheric polar vortex, the stratospheric final warming (SFW), and Southern Hemisphere climate trends. We first analyze ERA-Interim reanalysis and three climate model outputs with different climate forcings. Climate trends appear when there is a delay in the timing of SFWs. When regressed onto the SFW dates (which reflects the anomaly when the SFW is delayed for one standard deviation of its onset dates), the anomaly pattern bears a resemblance to the observed climate trends, for all the model outputs, even without any trends. This suggests that the stratospheric and tropospheric circulations are organized by the timing of SFWs, in both the interannual time scale and climate trends due to external forcings.We further explore the role of the SFW using a simplified dynamical model, in which the ozone depletion is mimicked by a springtime polar stratospheric cooling. The responses of zonal-mean atmospheric circ...

[Download paper](http://journals.ametsoc.org/doi/abs/10.1175/JAS-D-13-0290.1){target="_blank"}

:::

:::
